claim: Structural persistence in far-from-equilibrium physical and biological systems is explained by at least one of three independent sufficient conditions: (A) kinetic barrier dominance (E_a/δ >> 1), (B) active throughput dominance (M/δ > 1), or (C) critical-manifold tuning (system poised near divergence of correlation length ξ or susceptibility χ). Systems may instantiate multiple classes simultaneously (HYBRID). No single condition is universally necessary.
statement: Structural persistence in far-from-equilibrium physical and biological systems is explained by at least one of three independent sufficient conditions: (A) kinetic barrier dominance (E_a/δ >> 1), (B) active throughput dominance (M/δ > 1), or (C) critical-manifold tuning (system poised near divergence of correlation length ξ or susceptibility χ). Systems may instantiate multiple classes simultaneously (HYBRID). No single condition is universally necessary.
scope: DEPRECATED — trichotomy A/B/C is incomplete. At minimum two additional classes required: (D) TOPOLOGICAL discrete invariant, (E) NOISE_CONSTRUCTIVE structure. Also flagged: neutral persistence (evolutionary neutral networks), pattern persistence (intermediate disturbance), edge-of-stability (DNN training). Additionally: kernel-001 is descriptive taxonomy only — no generative predictions derivable from the trichotomy alone. Surviving content: Classes A/B/C intact in kernel-002.
assumptions: The system is demonstrably far from thermodynamic equilibrium.
At least one of E_a (Class A), M (Class B), or (ξ, χ) (Class C) is formally definable and measurable in the target domain.
The three conditions are logically independent: a system can instantiate any non-empty subset simultaneously.
No single condition is claimed to be universally necessary — the trichotomy provides sufficient conditions, not a unique decomposition.
formal_objects: E_a: kinetic barrier height — Class A
M: active maintenance capacity — Class B
Class C condition: ξ → ∞ or χ → ∞ near critical manifold
differentiators: DEPRECATED: trichotomy A/B/C incomplete — see docs/kernel-002_audit.md Phase 4 for break tests.
Class D countermodel (TOPOLOGICAL): driven-damped soliton with winding number Q persists with E_a/δ ≈ 1, M/δ < 1, ξ finite. Persistence via integer topological charge, not any of A/B/C.
Class E countermodel (NOISE_CONSTRUCTIVE): Horsthemke-Lefever noise-induced order; M and δ same physical process; M/δ ≡ 1 undefined. Class B not applicable.
Phase 3 audit: kernel-001 is descriptive taxonomy only — no novel predictions derivable beyond constituent theories (TST, ISS, RG) individually.
Phase 2 audit: A/B/C are mechanistically non-equivalent and non-reducible; Class C (RG) does not reduce to Lyapunov, basin geometry, FEP, or control theory.
Surviving content: Classes A/B/C are intact and correct within their scope; absorbed into kernel-002 as the first three of five classes.

break_tests.setup: A far-from-equilibrium system with E_a/δ ≈ 1 (no effective barrier), M/δ < 1 (no active throughput), and ξ finite / χ finite (not at criticality). Is structural persistence possible under kernel-001?
break_tests.expected: If the trichotomy covers all cases, no stability mechanism exists and structural collapse is predicted.
break_tests.observed: Topologically protected dissipative soliton (winding number Q=1): E_a/δ ≈ 1, M/δ < 1, ξ finite. Persists via discrete topological invariant. None of A/B/C operative.
break_tests.verdict: FAIL — Class D (TOPOLOGICAL) exists outside the trichotomy.

break_tests.setup: System where perturbation source and maintenance source are the same physical process (multiplicative noise). M/δ ≡ 1 by construction; M and δ not independently definable.
break_tests.expected: If Class B is universal for active systems, M/δ > 1 must be achievable.
break_tests.observed: Noise-induced order (Horsthemke-Lefever): structure at σ_opt > 0 collapses at σ = 0 and at σ >> σ_opt. M/δ is not operationalizable. Class B does not apply.
break_tests.verdict: FAIL — Class E (NOISE_CONSTRUCTIVE) exists outside the trichotomy.

break_tests.setup: A system persists structurally with E_a/δ ≈ 1 and M/δ < 1 and ξ, χ finite. Kernel-001 predicts collapse.
break_tests.expected: If trichotomy is exhaustive, no persistence is possible.
break_tests.observed: Topological systems (D) and noise-constructive systems (E) demonstrate persistence outside A/B/C.
break_tests.verdict: FAIL — Kernel-001 is incomplete. See kernel-002 for extension.

falsifiers: A far-from-equilibrium system demonstrably persisting structurally where all three conditions fail: E_a/δ ≈ 1, M/δ < 1, and ξ/χ finite — provided the system is not Class D or Class E.
A formal proof that any two of the three classes are logically equivalent or reducible to one another.
neighbors: Kramers escape rate / transition state theory — Class A
Self-organized criticality (Bak, Tang, Wiesenfeld 1987) — Class C
Stochastic resonance (Gammaitoni et al. 1998) — Class C / HYBRID
